constraints: Used shared local repo checkout in read-mostly mode
Did not edit tracked source files
Did not start services on ports 8081-8085

assertions.reason: `go test ./...` exited 0; `internal/server` passed and command packages reported no test files.

evidence.observed: ok github.com/yusefmosiah/go-choir/internal/server 1.442s; cmd/auth, cmd/gateway, cmd/proxy, cmd/sandbox, and cmd/vmctl reported [no test files].

assertions.reason: `pnpm build` exited 0 and produced `frontend/dist/index.html` plus built CSS/JS assets.

evidence.observed: Vite production build completed in 140ms and wrote dist/index.html, dist/assets/index-Boqa1hiR.css, and dist/assets/index-DU-_ztZq.js.

assertions.reason: Built `dist/index.html` is HTML and contains the text `go-choir` in the `<title>`.

evidence.command: python3 -c "from pathlib import Path; text=Path('dist/index.html').read_text(encoding='utf-8'); print('contains_go_choir=' + str('go-choir' in text.lower())); print('looks_like_html=' + str('<!doctype html' in text.lower() and '<html' in text.lower()))"

evidence.observed: The built HTML starts with <!DOCTYPE html> and includes <title>go-choir</title>.

assertions.reason: `nix flake show --all-systems` exited 0 and evaluated the flake successfully.

evidence.observed: Flake evaluation listed nixosConfigurations.go-choir-b and x86_64-linux packages auth, proxy, vmctl, gateway, sandbox, frontend, and default.

assertions.reason: This host cannot build the x86_64-linux package outputs required by the flake: the contract-style `.#auth`/`.#proxy`/... commands resolve against aarch64-darwin attrs and fail lookup, and explicit `#packages.x86_64-linux.<pkg>` retries fail because no x86_64-linux builder is available from this isolation context.

evidence.observed: Flake does not provide `packages.aarch64-darwin.auth`, `legacyPackages.aarch64-darwin.auth`, or top-level `auth` on this host.
Build reaches the x86_64-linux derivation but fails with `required system or feature not available`; available builder list does not include x86_64-linux.
Same x86_64-linux builder availability failure as auth.
Same x86_64-linux builder availability failure as auth.
Same x86_64-linux builder availability failure as auth.
Same x86_64-linux builder availability failure as auth.

assertions.reason: The frontend Nix package is also x86_64-linux-only in this flake, and this darwin host has no x86_64-linux builder available to produce the output path.

evidence.observed: Flake does not provide `packages.aarch64-darwin.frontend`, `legacyPackages.aarch64-darwin.frontend`, or top-level `frontend` on this host.
Explicit x86_64-linux retry fails with `required system or feature not available`; no x86_64-linux builder is configured here.

frictions.description: On this aarch64-darwin host, the contract-style `nix build .#auth` and `nix build .#frontend` commands fail attribute lookup because the flake only exports packages under `packages.x86_64-linux`.
frictions.resolution: Retried with explicit `#packages.x86_64-linux.<name>` attributes to confirm the real blocker.

blockers.description: No x86_64-linux build machine is available from this isolation context. Nix reports only an `aarch64-linux` remote builder and rejects all explicit x86_64-linux builds with `required system or feature not available`.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Retried `nix build --no-link --print-out-paths /Users/wiz/go-choir#packages.x86_64-linux.{auth,proxy,vmctl,gateway,sandbox,frontend}`; all exited 1 with the same builder-availability error.

summary: Tested 6 assigned assertions: 4 passed (VAL-SVC-008, VAL-FE-001, VAL-FE-002, VAL-NIX-001) and 2 were blocked by missing x86_64-linux build capacity in the local darwin environment (VAL-NIX-002, VAL-NIX-003).
